what_it_does: Implements window.MS_ARTIFACTS, an in-browser reactive store for produced design/code artifact instances. It persists artifacts to localStorage, validates kinds and statuses through window globals (MS_KINDS, MS_STATUS), resolves instance bindings via MS_RESOLVE, and exposes CRUD, status/content updates, listing, counts, and a React subscription hook. A seed routine creates demo artifacts when none are persisted.
observations: Uses localStorage key 'ms_artifacts_v1' to durably store an {_id, items} object.
Implements a simple subscriber Set; every mutating call runs persist() then notifies all subscribers.
create() validates kindId via MS_KINDS.get and status via MS_STATUS.get, resolves bindings/values/unresolved via MS_RESOLVE.instance, and picks a variant via MS_RESOLVE.variantBias.
Artifacts carry a contentStatus lifecycle field (pending|generating|ready|error) separate from approval status.
Provides a React hook useArtifacts() that forces re-render on store changes.
A self-executing seedArtifacts() block creates ten demo artifacts if localStorage is empty or missing.
standouts: Reactive localStorage persistence lets artifact changes appear live across Composition, Content, Vi console, etc.
Tight coupling to window globals MS_KINDS, MS_STATUS, MS_RESOLVE for validation and resolution.
Explicit variant storage so specimens render the same variant everywhere and decisions map to the right axis.
conventions: Namespace module attached to window as window.MS_ARTIFACTS.
IIFE returns a public API object and keeps internal state private.
Loud validation via console.error for unknown artifact/kind lookups.
Comments explicitly map the in-browser store to the SQL artifact + variable_binding tables.
concerns: Subscriber callback errors are silently swallowed in persist().
load() silently ignores JSON/localStorage failures and falls back to empty state.
create() returns null on unknown kind but does not roll back or report to caller beyond console.error.
Seed routine runs unconditionally at load time and depends on globals being present.
No localStorage quota or serialization size handling.
Exposes mutable internal _items array via the public API.
notes: This file is the runtime ledger: it turns kind definitions into persisted, reactive artifact rows and seeds a believable demo project on first load.
questions: What is the exact contract of MS_RESOLVE.instance and MS_RESOLVE.variantBias?
Which statuses are valid in MS_STATUS and how do they relate to contentStatus?
Is localStorage intended as the production persistence layer or only a development stand-in?
How is content actually generated and assigned via setContent?
What is the intended use of the uri field?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the comments describe it as a durable mirror of the artifact table with reactive updates and a demo seed, and the code implements exactly that.
apparent_themes: Runtime dependency injection through window globals
intention_signals: The file is trying to be the single source of truth for 'what the studio has actually made', so any surface can read or update artifacts and see changes immediately, while remaining runnable without a backend.
novelty: It treats generated design artifacts as first-class persisted instances with resolved variable bindings, a selected variant, and a separate content generation lifecycle, all synchronized through a browser-side pub/sub store.
contribution: A reactive, localStorage-backed artifact ledger with create/read/update/status/content operations, subscriber notifications, a React hook, and a demo seed; it gives the rest of the system a uniform runtime API for produced instances.
summary_for_embedding: artifacts.js implements window.MS_ARTIFACTS, a reactive browser-side store that persists design artifact instances to localStorage, validates them against MS_KINDS/MS_STATUS, resolves bindings via MS_RESOLVE, and exposes CRUD, status/content updates, counts, a React hook, and a demo seed routine.
intention_for_embedding: This file was written to provide a live, durable artifact inventory that bridges the Vi console, Composition gallery, and Review surfaces without requiring a backend.

symbols.does: Serializes the current _id counter and items array to localStorage and notifies all subscribers.
symbols.description: persist() is the reactive durability hook: it writes the entire artifact ledger under 'ms_artifacts_v1', then iterates the subscriber Set and invokes each callback so that any listening UI surface re-renders. It swallows localStorage and callback errors.

symbols.does: Registers a callback to be invoked on every store mutation and returns an unsubscribe function.
symbols.description: subscribe(fn) adds the callback to an internal Set and returns a closure that removes it. This is the primitive used by useArtifacts() and any other surface that wants live updates from the artifact ledger.

symbols.does: Restores the ledger from localStorage if a valid serialized object exists.
symbols.description: load() reads 'ms_artifacts_v1', parses it, and restores items and _id when the stored object has an Array items property. It returns true on success and false on any failure, leaving the store empty on error.

symbols.does: Creates a new artifact instance after validating kind and status and resolving bindings.
symbols.description: create(kindId, opts) looks up the kind in MS_KINDS, validates an optional status through MS_STATUS, calls MS_RESOLVE.instance to produce bindings/values/unresolved, selects a variant via MS_RESOLVE.variantBias or opts.variant, builds an artifact record with content/contentStatus fields, pushes it to items, persists, and returns the record. It logs an error and returns null for unknown kinds.

symbols.does: Assigns generated content to an artifact and updates its contentStatus.
symbols.description: setContent(id, content, status) finds the artifact by id, writes the content payload, sets contentStatus to the supplied status or defaults to 'ready' when content is truthy, persists, and returns the artifact. It returns null if the id is not found.

symbols.does: Updates only the content generation lifecycle status of an artifact.
symbols.description: setContentStatus(id, status) locates the artifact and sets its contentStatus field to the provided value, then persists. This lets callers mark content as pending, generating, ready, or error without changing the content payload itself.

symbols.does: Returns a filtered copy of the artifact array by kind and/or status.
symbols.description: list(filter) returns a new array containing artifacts whose kind_id and/or status match the optional filter criteria. With no filter it returns all artifacts.

symbols.does: Groups all artifacts into an object keyed by kind_id.
symbols.description: byKind() iterates items and builds a map from kind_id to an array of artifacts of that kind. This is useful for gallery or count UIs that want artifacts organized by their kind.

symbols.does: Retrieves a single artifact by id, logging an error if missing.
symbols.description: get(id) searches items for an artifact with the matching id and returns it. If not found it logs '[MS_ARTIFACTS] unknown artifact' to the console and returns null.

symbols.does: Changes the approval/workflow status of an artifact after validating it.
symbols.description: setStatus(id, status) finds the artifact, validates the new status through MS_STATUS.get when available, assigns it to a.status, persists, and returns the artifact. It returns null if the artifact does not exist.

symbols.does: Deletes an artifact from the ledger by id.
symbols.description: remove(id) finds the index of the artifact with the given id and, if present, splices it out of items and persists. It does not return the removed record and does not error if the id is missing.

symbols.does: Returns a tally of artifacts grouped by kind_id.
symbols.description: counts() iterates items and accumulates the number of artifacts per kind_id, returning an object like { kindId: n }. This supports gallery badges and summary widgets.

symbols.does: Helper used by the seed routine to create a demo artifact with project context and seed origin.
symbols.description: mk(kind, name, status, extra) is a closure inside seedArtifacts() that calls A.create with a shared projectCtx, the supplied name and status, origin 'seed', and any extra options. It reduces boilerplate for the ten demo artifacts created on first load.
